name: imagehash
description: SIMD perceptual hash (pHash) for near-duplicate image detection. A separable 2-D DCT is vectorized across 4 output frequencies with f32x4; JS has no vector type and runs the matrix multiply scalar. Fingerprints compare by Hamming distance.

alloc.doc: reserve byteLen bytes (8-byte aligned), growing linear memory; returns byte ptr

dct8x8.doc: OUT f32[64]: top-left 8x8 low-frequency DCT block for one 32x32 grayscale image

dctBatch.doc: OUT f32[64*count]: DCT block per image for a whole library in one call

phashBatch.doc: OUT u32[2*count]: 64-bit pHash per image (bit set where DCT coeff > block median)

memory.name: gray
memory.note: 32x32 grayscale per image (host: canvas downscale + Rec.601 luma)

notes: DCT intermediates are f32 (SIMD) vs f64 in a scalar-JS twin, so coefficients agree to ~1e-3 and the resulting perceptual hash is identical in practice. Image downscale/grayscale is native canvas work; only the DCT sweep runs in WASM.
